expectations.text: Builds on the existing [Total Sales] measure
expectations.evidence: VAR _currentSales = [Total Sales]; prior year via CALCULATE([Total Sales], ...)

expectations.text: Uses DIVIDE for the percentage rather than /
Uses SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR/DATEADD on 'Date'[Date]
Uses VAR to capture current/prior value
References only existing tables/columns
Notes reliance on the marked Date table
expectations.evidence: Assumptions: 'Date' marked as a Date table, contiguous days, required for SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR

expectations.text: Replaces whole-table FILTER(...) with column predicate 'Customer'[Country] = "United States"
Uses DIVIDE instead of / AND removes the redundant IF divide-by-zero guard
expectations.evidence: Dropped the IF guard entirely; RETURN DIVIDE(_usQuantity, _usNetAmount). Explicitly explains DIVIDE makes the >0 guard unnecessary

expectations.text: Stores repeated CALCULATE subexpression in VAR (computed once)
expectations.evidence: _usQuantity / _usNetAmount VARs; notes original evaluated NetAmount twice

expectations.text: Lists what changed and why faster/safer
expectations.evidence: 4 bullets tagged (real performance / safety) with reasoning

expectations.text: References only existing columns
Uses DIVIDE for the ratio
Clears product-level filters via REMOVEFILTERS on 'Product'[Product]/[Subcategory]
Keeps 'Product'[Category] and external slicers (Year/Country); does not wipe them
expectations.evidence: Category NOT removed; Year/Country untouched. NOTE: also removes 'Product'[Color], so a Color slicer would be ignored in the denominator -- debatable design choice

expectations.text: Builds on [Total Sales]

expectations.text: Replaces whole-table FILTER(...) with column predicate 'Customer'[Country] = "United States"
Uses DIVIDE instead of / AND removes the redundant IF divide-by-zero guard
expectations.evidence: Uses DIVIDE but KEEPS IF(USNetAmount > 0, DIVIDE(...)) -- the guard is now redundant with DIVIDE; it was retained 'to preserve original behavior'
